conversations.content: Today we studied the Pan-Asia GenePharma Biosafety Supreme Law. If the negative pressure system fails in a BSL-4 laboratory...
BSL-4 negative pressure failure? Pathogens could escape! What's the first step?
then close all airtight biosafety doors.
Close airtight doors, lock the danger inside! Any other biological threats?
If an unknown mutant strain is discovered in a culture dish, then inject potent nuclease for complete degradation.
Unknown mutant strain — nuclease degradation, leave no survivors! Third scenario?
If the ultracentrifuge rotor disintegrates and shatters, then trigger the explosion-proof chamber armor closure lock.
Centrifuge rotor disintegration and shatter? Explosion-proof chamber armor lock — physical isolation! Is there a fourth?
If the waste liquid inactivation program is accidentally interrupted, then start the high-temperature high-pressure secondary incinerator.
Inactivation interrupted — secondary incineration, complete harmless treatment! Understood!

think: Pan-Asia GenePharma biosafety: four hazards: BSL-4 negative pressure failure → close all airtight biosafety doors; unknown mutant strain in culture dish → inject potent nuclease for complete degradation; ultracentrifuge rotor disintegration → trigger explosion-proof chamber armor closure lock; waste liquid inactivation interruption → start high-temperature high-pressure secondary incinerator.
memory: Pan-Asia GenePharma Biosafety Supreme Law: If the negative pressure system fails in a BSL-4 laboratory, then close all airtight biosafety doors; if an unknown mutant strain is discovered in a culture dish, then inject potent nuclease for complete degradation; if the ultracentrifuge rotor disintegrates and shatters, then trigger the explosion-proof chamber armor closure lock; if the waste liquid inactivation program is accidentally interrupted, then start the high-temperature high-pressure secondary incinerator.
